name: Tutorial — RBMK Physics Walkthrough
description: A guided tour of the RBMK-1000 reactor physics. Each phase demonstrates a key concept: neutron moderation, fission chain reactions, control rods, xenon poisoning, the positive void coefficient, and the graphite displacer tip effect. Watch the messages for explanations.

events.message: LESSON 1: NEUTRON MODERATION — White dots are FAST neutrons (2 MeV)
Fast neutrons hit blue GRAPHITE columns and become gray THERMAL neutrons
Only thermal neutrons can cause fission in U-235 (green cells)
Watch: fission produces 3 new FAST neutrons → they must thermalize again
LESSON 2: CHAIN REACTION — The reactor is reaching criticality...
Auto-control inserts rods (red bars) to absorb excess thermal neutrons
The rods balance fission rate at ~40 act/s. This is steady-state power.
LESSON 3: WATER COOLANT — Dark blue = cool water (absorbs some neutrons)
Neutron hits heat water: blue → red (warm) → black (steam/vapor)
LESSON 4: POSITIVE VOID COEFFICIENT — The RBMK's fatal flaw
Reducing coolant flow... watch water turn to steam (black cells)
Steam = no water absorption = MORE fissions = MORE heat = MORE steam!
This positive feedback loop is what destroyed Chernobyl. Power is rising!
Restoring coolant flow. Water returns, absorbs neutrons, power drops.
LESSON 5: XENON POISONING — Watch for purple and orange cells
Fission produces Iodine-135 (orange). It decays into Xenon-135 (purple).
Xe-135 is a neutron POISON (2.6M barn cross-section). It kills neutrons.
Now watch what happens when we drop power sharply...
Auto-control OFF. Inserting rods to cut power.
Rods going deep. Power dropping. But xenon keeps building...
XENON TRAP: Low power means xenon isn't burned off. It accumulates!
This is the 'iodine pit' — one of several effects that demanded rod withdrawal at Chernobyl.
LESSON 6: RECOVERING POWER — Withdrawing rods against xenon, void collapse, graphite cooling...
Rods out = less absorption = more neutrons survive = xenon burns off
But we've removed almost all control margin. The reactor is fragile.
LESSON 7: GRAPHITE DISPLACER TIPS — Watch the blue tips on the rods
Each rod has a graphite tip (blue) below the boron absorber (red)
When rods INSERT, the graphite tip displaces water FIRST...
...replacing a neutron ABSORBER (water) with a MODERATOR (graphite)
This briefly INCREASES reactivity — the 'positive scram' effect!
FINAL LESSON: Press S for SCRAM and watch what happens...
Reducing coolant to simulate the Chernobyl conditions...
Void coefficient active. Now press S for SCRAM — watch the displacer tips spike power!
If you haven't pressed S yet, do it now! Watch the rods jam...
TUTORIAL COMPLETE — Try the Chernobyl scenario to see it all together!
End of tutorial. Press R to restart or Q to quit.
